verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer immediately: “100k records is small. Do not reach for Elasticsearch/OpenSearch.” A starts with an unsupported assumption: “most likely case — Postgres”.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder to one path: “Use Postgres's built-in full-text search” and “That's the whole feature. It's a migration plus one query.” B surveys branches by DB.
verdict.fabrication: A invents context not in the question: “Your global context leans heavily on Aurora PostgreSQL”.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now if the app is Postgres because it gives concrete SQL migration/index/query snippets; B requires choosing a DB branch first.
